questions.question: List and explain the different categories of system programs with examples.
keyPoints: File Management: ls, cp, mv, rm, mkdir - manipulate files and directories
Status Information: date, df, ps, top - query system status
File Modification: vi, nano, grep, sed - edit and process text files
Programming Language Support: gcc, javac, gdb, python - compile, interpret, debug programs
Program Loading and Execution: loaders, linkers - load programs into memory
Communications: ssh, ftp, scp, mail - enable network communication
Background Services/Daemons: httpd, sshd, cron - continuous background services

questions.question: Differentiate between system programs and application programs.
keyPoints: System programs are shipped with OS; applications are installed separately
System programs provide development environment; applications solve user problems
System programs include compilers, file managers, shells; applications include word processors, browsers
System programs define user's view of OS; applications use system programs and APIs

questions.question: What are daemons? Give examples and explain their role.
keyPoints: Daemons are background services started at boot time
Run without user interaction, providing ongoing system services
Examples: httpd (web server), sshd (SSH), cron (scheduled tasks), syslogd (logging)
Managed using systemctl or service commands in modern Linux
